sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the layout more clearly:
- White cross: rows 31-33, cols 20-22 (approximately)
- Blocks: rows 45-49, cols 19-23
- Between them: rows 34-44, which have 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGc' (rows 34-39) and 'ccccGGGGGcccccc' (rows 40-44)
Looking at the ascii output:
- Row 30: 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGc' - G floor, c wall at col 29
- Row 31: 'GGGGGGWGGGGGGGc' - White cross at col 21
- Row 32: 'GGGGGwWWGGGGGGc' - White cross at cols 20-22
- Row 33: 'GGGGGGwGGGGGGGc' - White cross at col 21
- Row 34: 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGc' - G floor
- Rows 35-39: 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGc' - G floor
- Rows 40-44: 'ccccGGGGGcccccc' - c walls at cols 15-18 and 24-29, G floor at cols 19-23
- Rows 45-46: 'ccccOOOOOGGGGGG' - Orange block at cols 19-23
- Rows 47-49: 'ccccbbbbbGGGGGG' - Blue block at cols 19-23
So the blocks are at cols 19-23, and the white cross is at cols 20-22. The blocks are directly below the white cross, in the same column range.
The path from the blocks to the white cross is:
- Rows 45-49: blocks at cols 19-23
- Rows 40-44: 'ccccGGGGGcccccc' - G floor at cols 19-23, c walls at cols 15-18 and 24-29
- Rows 35-39: 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGc' - G floor at cols 15-28, c wall at col 29
- Rows 31-33: white cross at cols 20-22
So the blocks can move UP through the corridor at cols 19-23 to reach the white cross. The corridor is 5 cells wide (cols 19-23), and the blocks are 5 cells wide. So the blocks should be able to move UP.
Let me try moving the blocks UP to see if they can reach the white cross.
The blocks are at rows 45-49, and the white cross is at rows 31-33. The distance is about 12-14 cells. Let me move UP by 14 cells.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at rows 45-49, white cross at rows 31-33
# Need to move UP by about 14 cells to reach the white cross
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
